# ClawPilot / Project Lobster Signal

This note incorporates the [local ClawPilot import brief](../../import/clawpilot.md) as the local source-of-record for the sidebar. The shared ChatGPT research thread remains a prompted input and context trail, not final evidence on its own:
[shared ChatGPT thread](https://chatgpt.com/s/t_6a01debc15888191acf04212b95dd8b2).

## Narrative Use

Use this as a short "where agents are heading" sidebar in the HMRC talk. The useful point is not a product prediction; it is the enterprise pattern: persistent agents need identity, permissions, channels, observability, revocation, and evidence.

The local brief's most important distinction is that "ClawPilot" can refer to Microsoft's internal Project Lobster work and also to unrelated public/commercial Clawpilot services. Do not collapse those into one product claim.

## Evidence To Treat As Stronger

- The committed import brief records the working synthesis, source list, and public-sector evaluation lens: [local ClawPilot import brief](../../import/clawpilot.md).
- Omar Shahine's own write-up says he announced a Microsoft role focused on bringing OpenClaw and personal agents to Microsoft 365, and frames the lessons as harness, memory, security, graduated autonomy, identity, integration depth, proactive behaviour, and lived use: [Lobster Got Hired](https://www.omarknows.ai/p/lobster-got-hired).
- Microsoft Learn describes Microsoft Entra Agent ID as the identity and access foundation for Microsoft Agent 365, with governance of agent lifecycles, access, and audit activity: [Microsoft Entra Agent ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/agent-id/).
- Microsoft 365 admin documentation says some agent instances receive their own Entra-backed identity, licence, mailbox, OneDrive, and Teams presence: [Understand agent details](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/manage/agent-details?view=o365-worldwide#agent-instances).
- Microsoft's Agent 365 general availability post frames the enterprise problem as observing, governing, and securing agents across local, SaaS, and cloud environments, including local agents such as OpenClaw: [Microsoft Agent 365 GA post](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2026/05/01/microsoft-agent-365-now-generally-available-expands-capabilities-and-integrations/).

## Evidence To Treat As Weaker

- The "ClawPilot" and "Project Lobster" labels, and internal-adoption numbers such as more than 3,000 daily Microsoft users by 1 May 2026, appear in secondary reporting and in the shared ChatGPT research thread. Treat these as "reported" until checked against a primary Microsoft or Omar Shahine source immediately before the talk.

## Talk Integration

- Link this to the talk's main boundary: as agents gain persistent identity and richer tool access, governance becomes part of the engineering product.
- Use it to explain why source-backed answers, audit trails, and review gates matter before public-sector teams move from coding assistants to agents that can act.
- Avoid presenting unverified internal Microsoft usage numbers as settled fact.
- Use the public-sector evaluation lens from the import brief: identity, least privilege, human approval, auditability, data governance, prompt-injection defence, supply chain, and recovery.
